channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Tue, 21 Jul 2026 13:54:03 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Landlord firm fined £42,500 after tenants found living among rats and human waste
item.description: A landlord company in County Durham has been fined £42,500 after tenants were found living in unsafe conditions, including sleeping among dead rats, mice, human waste and urine. The court heard the firm failed to carry out required property inspections and maintain valid gas safety certificates across several rental homes, prompting legal action by Durham County Council.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: West London streets flooded annually with heavy rains: Five little beavers solved the problem for free by building an extensive dam network
item.description: Ealing borough in London has seen a successful return of beavers, who are tackling flooding issues head-on. By creating wetlands, these industrious animals have managed to retain excess rainwater during intense storms. Remarkably, the area surrounding Greenford station has remained safeguarded from flooding for three years. Additionally, this initiative has revitalized local ecosystems, drawing in diverse wildlife and exemplifying how natural interventions can solve pressing environmental problems.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: A 1,740-year-old Roman coin was found during an English railway dig, revealing a hidden world beneath the ground
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Florida confirms Burmese python breeding hotspot outside the Everglades, and wildlife experts fear it could be the start of a bigger spread
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: As temperatures soar, dozens of donkeys in UK are wearing pyjamas and 'don-gerees' to stop painful insect bites, and the clever reason is winning hearts
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The largest male great white shark ever tagged in the Atlantic has resurfaced after travelling 7,000 miles; scientists say his journey is far from over
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by David Beckham: "I have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to…" - a powerful lesson on hard work, discipline and why talent alone is never enough
item.description: David Beckham's quote highlights continuous hard work as essential for special achievements. He stressed that consistent effort, not just occasional bursts, builds lasting success. Talent provides a head start, but dedication often surpasses natural ability over time. Beckham's career, including overcoming setbacks, exemplifies this philosophy in action. Modern culture often portrays success as instant, but real achievements develop quietly through dedication.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is Jelicia Westhoff? Maduka Okoye’s baby mama accuses Nigerian goalkeeper of cheating and abuse amid Cardi B rumours
item.description: Jelicia Westhoff has found herself in the spotlight after making a series of serious claims against Maduka Okoye. Her posts appeared shortly after the Nigerian goalkeeper was linked to Cardi B following public sightings in Italy. Here's a closer look at who Westhoff is, her relationship with Okoye, and what led to the latest controversy.
item.dc:creator: Global Sports Desk

item.title: Mediators propose 10-day truce to revive interim deal: Iran official
item.description: Iran received a 10-day ceasefire proposal from mediators to end the ongoing conflict. US strikes on Iranian cities continued for a ninth consecutive night. Iran's Revolutionary Guards attacked US military assets across the region. Two oil tankers exploded after attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz. A vessel was struck by an unknown projectile off Oman's coast.

item.title: Have demanded extradition of Hasina aide from India: Bangladesh minister
item.description: Bangladesh has requested India to extradite former minister Asaduzzaman Khan Kamal. He fled the country amid the "2024 uprising" against the government. Kamal was sentenced to death for crimes against humanity by a tribunal. Dhaka also expects India to respond to extradition requests for other fugitives. The government stated that Hasina would face legal consequences if she surrenders.
item.dc:creator: Ahsan Tasnim

item.title: Who is TheMightyBA? Ludwig remains hopeful despite Streamer University 2026 controversy
item.description: TheMightyBA came under fire after his interaction with MeesterKeem at Streamer University 2026. Despite the backlash, Ludwig said he does not believe the 19-year-old creator’s career is over. He said people can change over time and used IShowSpeed as an example of someone who successfully rebuilt his public image through growth and consistency.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Looksmaxxing creator Hullo reportedly returns to TikTok after 15 CSAM charges
item.description: Mason Hull, known online as Hullo, has reportedly resumed posting on TikTok. This comeback follows his recent charges for child sexual abuse material possession. Many social media users are discussing his return and platform policies. His legal case remains ongoing, with no verdict reached yet. The creator's online presence continues to spark debate among the public.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Asmongold’s girlfriend Kaise finds cockroach head in streamer’s fridge during live stream
item.description: Popular streamer Asmongold's girlfriend Kaise discovered a cockroach head in his refrigerator. The unexpected find occurred during a recent livestream while preparing food together. Asmongold casually explained the possible accidental presence of the insect part. This incident added to ongoing discussions about the streamer's home condition. Kaise's visit continues to provide memorable moments for viewers.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Quote of the day by Aeschylus: 'When one is wise, it's wisest to seem foolish' and a lesson on strategic survival
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the Day by Seneca: 'We suffer more often in imagination than in reality'
item.description: Seneca, the ancient Stoic philosopher, observed that our imagination often causes us more suffering than actual events. His timeless insight resonates with issues of anxiety faced today. Contemporary research shows our brain struggles to differentiate between real threats and imagined ones. Cognitive Behavioral Therapy leverages this understanding to help individuals challenge distorted thoughts, enabling them to tackle fears more effectively and manage anxiety around potential issues.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Epicurus: 'Death does not concern us, because as long as we exist...'
item.description: Epicurus, the ancient Greek philosopher, offered a compelling perspective on the fear of death, asserting that it should not concern us since our existence ceases when death arrives. This notion, termed the symmetry argument, challenges the fears associated with dying. Residing in a community known as The Garden, Epicurus sought to promote tranquility through his teachings, which continue to resonate today amidst contemporary worries about life and health.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Usha Vance: 'There's nothing more exciting than getting to see the world through the eye of kids as they're growing up' and the joys of motherhood
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists tracked endangered Australian cockatoos for three breeding seasons and found...
item.description: The peril faced by Carnaby's black cockatoos is escalating, as pesticide contamination in their food reaches alarming rates. Testing of seed samples revealed that toxic chemicals present are hundreds of times higher than what is legally permissible. This exposure might shed light on the troubling paralysis syndrome afflicting these endangered birds. Furthermore, researchers identified neurotoxic substances banned in various regions of Europe.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Lamine Yamal: "My mother had me at 16, and my father searched for ways to bring food home. That's real pressure. All I have to do is…" - a powerful lesson on perspective, family sacrifice and why football is not the hardest battle his family has faced
item.description: Teenage footballer Lamine Yamal shared his perspective on pressure during the World Cup build-up. He contrasted his career with his mother's early parenthood and father's past hardships. Yamal views his demanding football career as a privilege compared to their struggles. This humility places his own challenges within a broader family context. His words offer a powerful reflection on gratitude and perspective beyond the sport.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Tina Sodhi paid $2,000 for wellness treatment in Florida, she never made it home; doctor charged with manslaughter a year later
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
